definition: Use chromosome or genomic interval membership as a coarse generalization axis. Same chromosome or nearby genomic intervals are high-overlap; held-out chromosomes or far genomic intervals are lower-overlap evaluation units.
example_usage: python tabular_group_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --group-col chromosome

citations.title: DART-Eval: A Comprehensive DNA Language Model Evaluation Benchmark on Regulatory DNA

evidence.summary: The paper defines a train/validation/test split using held-out chromosomes for regulatory DNA evaluation.

limitations: Chromosome identity is coarse and does not measure local regulatory syntax.
Same-chromosome intervals can still be biologically unrelated.
Held-out chromosomes can confound sequence composition, annotation density, and cell-type coverage.
quality_gates: Report chromosome membership for train, validation, and test.
Also audit sequence composition, motif content, and regulatory element class.
Avoid mixing interval-level and variant-level units without explicit identifiers.
